repair_candidate.summary: Repair contributor PR #77539 in place: keep the narrow task override fix for subagent replacement runs, rebase/refresh against current main if needed, resolve or document stale Copilot comments, and rerun changed-surface validation. repair_candidate.pr_title: fix(subagent): preserve steered task text on restart redispatch
repair_candidate.pr_body:
## What Problem This Solves

When a subagent run is steered, woken, orphan-resumed, or reactivated from a completed child session, a gateway restart can redispatch from the persisted subagent run record. Current main keeps the old `task` on replacement runs, so restart recovery can rewrap and replay stale pre-steer text instead of the prompt that was actually dispatched.

## Why This Change Was Made

This repairs the existing contributor PR #77539 in place. The branch should persist an optional replacement `task` through `replaceSubagentRunAfterSteer`, pass the dispatched prompt from steer/wake/completed-session paths, and keep orphan recovery using the stable original task rather than accumulating nested resume wrappers.

## User Impact

Subagent follow-up and steer behavior survives gateway restarts without losing the latest user instruction. Contributor credit remains with @amittell and the source PR history.

## Evidence

- Current main `5e4d71686a6a1745635a7e1ad6caf756ab8a94bf` lacks a replacement task override in `src/agents/subagent-registry-run-manager.ts` and related callers.
- PR head `9889feca8454b28fdf9c15fb93f044eb3c0901eb` adds the task override and focused regression coverage.
- Run focused subagent/gateway tests plus `pnpm check:changed` after any rebase or repair.
- Rerun Codex `/review` separately and resolve any actionable bot/human findings before a merge-capable lane considers landing.
credit_notes: Preserve @amittell as the source contributor on https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/77539.
Keep repair work on the existing PR branch when possible; do not replace unless re-fetch shows maintainer_can_modify=false, broad unrelated churn, or a changed unsafe branch state.
